name: Exchange Bank of Missouri
description_short: Exchange Bank of Missouri is a community bank offering personal and business checking, savings, loans, and mortgage services across multiple Missouri locations.
description_long: Exchange Bank of Missouri is a regional bank serving Missouri communities with a range of traditional banking products and services. The bank operates multiple physical branch locations and emphasizes personalized customer service through dedicated loan officers and community-focused lending. The bank offers comprehensive personal banking services including interest-bearing checking accounts, savings accounts, certificates of deposit, individual retirement accounts, and safe deposit boxes. On the lending side, Exchange Bank provides auto loans, personal loans, home equity loans, debt consolidation, mortgage loans, and credit cards. For business customers, they offer business checking, cash management services, and business loans designed to support local business development. Exchange Bank distinguishes itself through emphasis on personalized service, with loan officers available for consultations and multiple physical branch locations for in-person banking. They have invested in digital accessibility (AudioEye enabled website) and mobile banking solutions, including mobile check deposit and a mobile app available on iPhone, iPad, and Android platforms. The bank positions itself as community-focused, with messaging around supporting local business growth and helping customers achieve financial goals. As a regional community bank, Exchange Bank of Missouri provides traditional banking services with local presence and relationship-based lending. However, the website provides limited specific information about fees, interest rates, loan terms, or competitive advantages. The company is FDIC insured and an Equal Housing Lender, indicating regulatory compliance, but detailed product specifications and pricing remain unavailable from the website content alone.
services: Personal checking accounts with interest-bearing options
Savings accounts and certificates of deposit
Mobile banking and mobile check deposit
pros: FDIC insured member bank providing deposit protection up to regulatory limits
Multiple physical branch locations for in-person banking and service
Mobile banking app available on iPhone, iPad, and Android with mobile check deposit
Dedicated loan officers available for personalized mortgage and business loan consultations
Comprehensive product suite including checking, savings, CDs, IRAs, auto loans, mortgages, and business loans
Interest-bearing checking accounts available for personal customers
Online mortgage pre-qualification tools (Home BuyAbility Test) and credit card qualification assessment
cons: Website lacks specific information about fee schedules, interest rates, or APR ranges for any products
Limited details on loan approval criteria, credit requirements, or terms for personal and auto loans
No information about minimum deposit requirements, account minimums, or product-specific benefits comparison
Regional bank with geographic limitation to Missouri locations only
Website does not disclose business loan rates, SBA lending options, or equipment financing details
best_for: Missouri residents seeking community-based banking with local branch access
Small business owners needing relationship-based lending and cash management services
Borrowers preferring in-person consultations with dedicated loan officers for mortgages and business loans
Customers wanting FDIC-insured deposits with mobile and online banking convenience
diagnosis: Exchange Bank of Missouri is best for Missouri residents and small business owners who value local branch access and relationship-based banking with dedicated loan officers. The main caveat is that the website provides minimal transparency on pricing, rates, fees, and specific loan terms—prospective customers must contact the bank directly at 1-888-797-3388 or visit a branch location to compare products competitively.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
